summary: Moved the DownWait portion of ftCommon down-bound/down-wait logic out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and into src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DownBound.c, adding C implementations and prototypes for ftCo_80097E8C, ftCo_80097F38, ftCo_DownWait_Anim, ftCo_DownWait_IASA, ftCo_DownWait_Phys, and ftCo_DownWait_Coll. The PR partially matched the group: the report shows ftCo_80097E8C and all DownWait callbacks at 100%, while ftCo_80097F38 remained at 96.03%. It also improved supporting types by adding a downwait motion-var field at fp+2340, exposing ftCommonData->x424, tightening several prototypes, and switching some functions from HSD_GObj* to ftCo_GObj*.
what_changed: Removed 169 lines of standalone assembly for ftCo_80097E8C, ftCo_80097F38, ftCo_DownWait_Anim, ftCo_DownWait_IASA, ftCo_DownWait_Phys, and ftCo_DownWait_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.

Added C-side DownWait implementations in ftCo_DownBound.c, with inline asm fallback blocks under defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP) for the functions that still needed exact preservation in normal matching builds.

Implemented ftCo_80097E8C as a transition into DownWait: it checks GA_Air to call ftCommon_8007D7FC, selects ftCo_MS_DownWaitU versus ftCo_MS_DownWaitD based on current motion, initializes fp->mv.co.downwait.x0 from p_ftCommonData->x424, calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState with skip/keep flags, then calls ftAnim_8006EBA4 and ftCommon_8007E2F4.
Implemented ftCo_80097F38 as a DownDamage-to-DownWait transition using ftCo_MS_DownDamageU to choose ftCo_MS_DownWaitU versus ftCo_MS_DownWaitD, followed by animation setup and ftCommon_8007E2F4.

Implemented ftCo_DownWait_Anim as a timer countdown using fp->mv.co.downwait.x0, gated by !fp->x2224_flag.bits.b2, and transitioning through ftCo_80098160 to ftCo_MS_DownStandU or ftCo_MS_DownStandD when the timer reaches zero.
Implemented ftCo_DownWait_IASA with sequential RETURN_IF checks for ftCo_800984D4, ftCo_80098214, and ftCo_800980BC.
Implemented ftCo_DownWait_Phys as a thin wrapper around ft_80084F3C and ftCo_DownWait_Coll as a thin wrapper around ft_80083F88.
Added ftCommon_MotionVars.downwait with a float at fp+2340, matching the assembly access to offset 0x2340.
Split ftCommonData padding around +0x420 and identified +0x424 as float x424, used as the DownWait timer source.

Updated header prototypes: ftCo_800980BC and ftCo_800984D4 became bool ftCo_GObj* functions, ftCo_80098160 became void ftCo_80098160(ftCo_GObj*, FtMotionId), and DownWait prototypes were added to ftCo_DownBound.h.
Changed nearby ftCo_DownBound.c function signatures from HSD_GObj* to ftCo_GObj* for ftCo_800976A4, ftCo_8009794C, and ftCo_80097AF4.
smart_moves: Used the existing motion-id naming to replace raw motion constants: DownBoundU/DownSpotU map to DownWaitU, other cases map to DownWaitD; DownWaitU maps to DownStandU in the timer expiry path, otherwise DownStandD.
Recovered a shared motion variable rather than leaving raw fp+2340 accesses: fp->mv.co.downwait.x0 documents the timer-like value used by DownWait_Anim.
Recovered p_ftCommonData->x424 as the source for the DownWait timer initialization, replacing an opaque load from +0x424 in the assembly.

Kept exact inline assembly under MUST_MATCH for the harder or build-sensitive pieces while still landing readable C under the alternate path, allowing incremental progress despite ftCo_80097F38 not fully matching.
Converted simple callbacks to idiomatic wrappers, making Phys and Coll trivial and searchable while preserving their original call targets.
Tightened prototypes in ft_08A4.h, which reduces UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS leakage and supports future callers with correct return types and FtMotionId arguments.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter action uses fp+2340, check the ftCommon_MotionVars union before inventing a global or local; this PR models that slot as mv.co.downwait.x0.
DownWait action state is controlled by a countdown initialized from ftCommonData +0x424, decremented unless x2224_flag.bits.b2 is set, then transitions to DownStandU/DownStandD.
For action transitions, compare current motion IDs to choose U/D variants instead of preserving raw numeric constants; this made the C much clearer and mapped directly to the assembly branches.
RETURN_IF chains are a concise match-friendly pattern for IASA routines that call several bool helpers in order and stop on the first handled input.
Thin Phys/Coll callbacks often just delegate to shared fighter helpers; here DownWait_Phys delegates to ft_80084F3C and DownWait_Coll delegates to ft_80083F88.
Tightening GObj typedefs from HSD_GObj* to ftCo_GObj* can unlock cleaner user_data access and more accurate headers without changing behavior.
Partial PRs can still be valuable if most functions become C and the remaining mismatch is isolated with MUST_MATCH assembly.
naming_conventions: Use ftCo_DownWait_Anim, ftCo_DownWait_IASA, ftCo_DownWait_Phys, and ftCo_DownWait_Coll for common fighter action callbacks.
Use ftCo_GObj* for common fighter GObj parameters where the function accesses ftCo_Fighter data through gobj->user_data.
Use ftCo_Fighter* fp for fighter user_data variables.
Use FtMotionId msid for local motion-state choices.
Use ftCo_MS_* enum names for common motion IDs such as ftCo_MS_DownWaitU, ftCo_MS_DownWaitD, ftCo_MS_DownStandU, and ftCo_MS_DownStandD.
Unknown but structurally placed common data fields can remain xNNN names, as with ftCommonData.x424, while still being typed as float when proven by loads/stores.
Motion-variable union members should describe the action namespace when known, as with mv.co.downwait.x0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed standalone assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and colocated the decompiled functions in the relevant ftCommon C file.
Used #if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP) inline asm blocks with nofralloc for functions that still needed exact assembly preservation.
Used C alternatives beneath the asm guards so matching reports could evaluate readable decompiled logic while preserving exact output in strict builds.
Replaced raw floating constants from sdata loads with NULL, 0, 1 arguments to Fighter_ChangeMotionState where the C semantics were understood.
Preserved the original motion-state flags as a named OR combination: Ft_MF_SkipModel | Ft_MF_SkipMatAnim | Ft_MF_SkipNametagVis | Ft_MF_KeepColAnimPartHitStatus.
The report evidence shows ftCo_80097E8C, ftCo_DownWait_Anim, ftCo_DownWait_IASA, ftCo_DownWait_Phys, and ftCo_DownWait_Coll at 100%, while ftCo_80097F38 remained slightly mismatched at 96.03%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
There is no explicit reviewer rationale in the available evidence; conclusions are based on the title, changed files, diff, and matching report.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ftCo_80097F38 after this 96.03% partial match.
Search for other users of ftCommonData.x424 to determine the semantic name of the DownWait timer constant.
Search for mv.co.downwait.x0 or fp+2340 in later ftCommon work to see if the field was renamed from x0.
Inspect later changes to ftCo_80098160, ftCo_800980BC, ftCo_80098214, and ftCo_800984D4 for prototype stabilization and naming improvements.
Search for additional HSD_GObj* to ftCo_GObj* conversions in ftCommon action files.
